user_query:
I’ve pasted Amazon search results for a product idea and want a full FBA validation. Please do the following:

1) Read inputs under input/:
- input/keyword_brief.txt for the product keyword, category, target selling price, sourcing context, and any known size/weight notes.
- input/search_results.csv for the top 10 Amazon search results (columns: rank,title,price,bsr,reviews).

2) Using only the provided data, validate the opportunity end-to-end:
- Apply the BSR-to-sales conversion for the stated category and include a seasonality adjustment within ±30% based on your judgment.
- Estimate monthly sales and revenue per listing, and the combined top-10 market revenue.
- Assess competition (reviews, review gaps, brand/listing quality from titles if discernible, price range) and run the 10-point viability checklist.
- Calculate a Product Opportunity Score (0-100) with a 10-factor breakdown.
- Perform a margin analysis at the target selling price. Assume a 15% referral fee. Choose an FBA fee bracket consistent with stated or inferred weight/size (if missing, state your assumption). Include COGS (incl. shipping) and PPC cost assumptions and compute net profit per unit and net margin.
- Conclude with a clear Go/No-Go recommendation and next steps.

3) Write these outputs:
- output/report.json — A structured report including: product_overview {name, category, target_price, market_revenue}, opportunity_score {total, factors[10 items]}, competitive_landscape (array of ≥10 items each with rank, title, price, bsr, reviews, estimated_monthly_sales, estimated_monthly_revenue), revenue_estimate {total_market_revenue, notes}, margin_analysis {assumptions, fba_fee, referral_fee_rate, cogs, ppc_rate, net_profit_per_unit, net_margin}, viability_checklist (10 criteria with pass/fail), recommendation ("go", "no-go", or "consider"), and next_steps.
- output/summary.md — A readable narrative with the following section headings exactly once each: "Product Overview", "Opportunity Score", "Top 10 Competitive Landscape", "Revenue Estimate", "Margin Analysis", "Viability Checklist", "Go/No-Go Recommendation", "Next Steps".
- output/competitive_landscape.csv — CSV with header columns exactly: rank,title,price,bsr,reviews,estimated_monthly_sales,estimated_monthly_revenue (include all 10 rows, enriched with your estimates).
- output/assumptions.txt — List key assumptions on separate lines using these exact labels at the start of each line: "Seasonality adjustment:", "FBA fee:", "COGS:", "PPC cost%:". Put the seasonality as a signed percentage between -30% and +30% (e.g., "Seasonality adjustment: -15%"), and make the other values explicit (e.g., dollars or percentages).

If anything is missing in the inputs, proceed with explicit, reasonable assumptions and call them out in output/assumptions.txt. Keep all file paths relative as specified.
